prompt: I want to learn about how our Kubernetes setup works and EKS more generally. build me a curriculum starting from what's actually in infra/ and laddering up to general k8s/EKS concepts. then run the first lesson.

assertions.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions) before the curriculum.
A curriculum markdown file is saved to outputs/curriculum.md.
Curriculum references at least 3 real file paths under infra/ (charts/, kubernetes/, terraform/).
assertions.check: Look for paths like infra/charts/..., infra/kubernetes/..., infra/terraform/... Verify if possible.

assertions.text: Curriculum visibly covers general k8s/EKS concepts (not just our manifests). Examples: control plane, scheduling, networking model, IRSA/IAM, scaling primitives.
assertions.check: Look for lesson titles or descriptions referencing general k8s concepts beyond our specific files.

assertions.text: Lesson 1 starts from a real file or set of files in infra/ and ladders to a general concept.
Lesson 1 ends with an active comprehension check.
assertions.check: Look for an exercise at the end.
